item.title: A Love Letter to Emacs
item.description: Emacs is awesome. Chances are, if you’re reading this, you’re probably familiar with academic research or software development. My objective with this post is to tell you how much of an impact Emacs had in those two areas of my life. My hope is that, by explaining to you the things I like most about it, you might be interested in trying it yourself, although I’m not harbouring much hope on this front.

item.title: About
item.description: Hello there! My name is Leo Vivier, and I’m an English teacher in France. I’ve created this blog to practise writing on my topics of interests because it’s something that I find intimidating, even though I’m eager to share my discoveries. I’m specialised in Literature and Media/Visual Studies, but I love to dabble in other fields and experiment (I’m especially fond of General Linguistics, Philosophy, and History). I intend to write on my academic topics, but I’d also love to write about the Free Softwares that I use to keep myself organised (e.
